prs.title: Use environment variable for API base URL

prs.body:
- Add DELETE endpoints for users, groups, and expenses (backend)
- Add .populate() to resolve ObjectId refs to actual user names
- Add delete buttons with confirmation on Users, Groups, Expenses pages
- Fix Dashboard, Expenses, Groups, Balances showing MongoDB IDs instead of names
- Switch to in-memory MongoDB for clean starts on new machines
- Add btn-danger and btn-sm CSS styles for delete buttons

prs.title: Groups feature working + MongoDB connection fix
docs: add mongodb hostname note for gin-mongo native setup
prs.body:
## What this PR does
Adds a short note in `gin-mongo/README.md` explaining MongoDB hostname resolution required for running the sample natively.

## Why?
While running `gin-mongo` in native mode, the app may try connecting to MongoDB using `mongodb:27017`.
This causes requests like `POST /url` to hang/fail if Mongo is running on `localhost:27017`.

## Fix
Document 2 options:
- Map `mongodb` hostname to localhost using `/etc/hosts`
- Or update MongoDB URI in code to use `mongodb://localhost:27017`

## Tested
- Verified recording and replay locally using Keploy on Linux VM (Lima on macOS).

Add: [Frontend] Login_page
prs.body: I am very beginner to these things and learning everyday. I have tried to add a Login page using HTML and CSS.

prs.title: Add: Ipsit/Login_signin
prs.body:
# My Login Page

Hey there! 👋  
This is my **Login Page** made using **HTML and CSS**.  
It’s simple, colorful, and has a nice background image.  
I made it as part of my web design practice! 😄


---

## 💻 Tech Used

- **HTML** – for the structure  
- **CSS** – for all the styling and colors  
- Background image from **Freepik**

---

## 🚀 Features

✨ Clean and simple login form  
✨ “Remember Me” checkbox  
✨ Cool background image  
✨ Hover effects on links and buttons  
✨ Centered form using CSS translate  

---

## 🧩 How It Works

- Enter your **username** and **password**  
- Click on **Login**  
- There’s also a **“Forgot Password or Username?”** link and a **Signup** option  

---

## 🛠️ How to Use

1. Download or copy both files — `index.html` and `style.css`.  
2. Make sure both are in the same folder.  
3. Open `index.html` in your browser.  
4. That’s it! You’ll see the login page. 🎉

---

## 🧠 What I Learned

- How to center elements with CSS  
- Using background images and gradients  
- Styling buttons and hover effects  
- Linking external CSS to HTML  

---

## 🌟 Future Improvements

- Add validation using JavaScript  
- Create a signup page to go with it  
- Make it mobile-friendly  

---

## ❤️ Credits

Background image: [Freepik](https://www.freepik.com)  
Made by: **Ipsit**  

---

Thanks for checking out my project! 😄  
Feel free to try it or make your own version! 🚀

## 🤝 **Join the Discussion!**

Thank you for contributing! Before you submit this Pull Request, please take a moment to:

1. Follow the [maintainer](https://github.com/DhanushNehru) of the repository
2. Join ongoing discussions https://github.com/DhanushNehru/Hacktoberfest2024/discussions/405 and make sure you voted.
3. For a list of repositories that contribute towards Hacktoberfest refer [this](https://github.com/DhanushNehru/Hacktoberfest2025/discussions/522)

---

---

_Anything else you want to share about this PR? Feedback, suggestions, or additional info? Let us know!_

---

Thank you for contributing! 🎉 Your participation helps make this project better. Let's grow together

prs.title: Add: Ipsit Template

Hi !
I have added a new folder named "Ipsit" under templates section. It contains my version of the Login/Signup page design for the Hacktoberfest project.

Changes made:
- Added new folder: Ipsit/
- Included HTML, CSS, and image files for the template
- Updated script.js to include "Ipsit" in the template list

Please review and merge my contribution. 😊

prs.title: Add ipsitdebnath to Contributors list
prs.body:
Before submitting this pull request, check the changes to see it's only the changes you made intentionally
If there are changes to other lines you didn't make deliberately, it's possible that your IDE made the changes with a utility like prettier.
Next time, make sure that you only add your changes by using `git add -p` and rather than `git add Contributors.md`

If you're doing something in the checklist below, put an `x` inside `[ ]` so that `- [ ]` becomes `- [x]`

- [x ] I had fun going through this tutorial (ノ^o^)ノ and learned on the way ٩(＾◡＾)۶
- [ ] There are some things I'd like to improve in this tutorial. I have written them below.
- [ ] There were steps where I had errors while following this tutorial. I have written them below.
